text.title: ADA Title II Compliance 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the ADA Title II Compliance 2026 methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Conformance target is WCAG 2.1 AA across all in-scope content. Higher targets (2.2 AA, AAA) are allowed but the rule floor is 2.1 AA.

rationale.source: ada.gov / DOJ Title II Final Rule 2024

rule.rationale: DOJ explicitly cites WCAG 2.1 Level AA in the 2024 Title II rule.

rule.statement: Entities with population ≥50,000 (or special purpose districts of similar scale) MUST conform by April 24, 2026; smaller entities by April 26, 2027.
rule.rationale: Population thresholds are codified in the rule text.

rule.statement: Exceptions (archived content, third-party content meeting conditions, individualised documents, conventional electronic documents from non-entity sources, social-media posts pre-effective-date) MUST be enumerated with rationale per item.
rule.rationale: DOJ requires that exceptions be specific; blanket 'we apply all exceptions' is non-compliant.

rule.statement: Each service area in the inventory names a single accountable owner (handle + role). 'Team' / 'gov' / empty are forbidden.
rule.rationale: OCR enforcement contacts a named owner; anonymous areas accumulate.

rule.statement: Any service area with conformance verdict != pass MUST link a remediation plan with milestones tied to the relevant deadline.
rule.rationale: DOJ expects a documented path to compliance, not just a verdict.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ada-title-ii-compliance-2026.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
